title: Mottainai Baasan
titleNative: もったいないばあさん
synopsis: In the books, the titular Grandma seeks to explore the true meaning of the Japanese phrase "mottainai" after her son asked her what it meant and she couldn't answer. "Mottainai" is a commonly used exclamation that roughly translates to "what a waste!", expressing regret over wastefulness. The Grandma seeks to explore the meaning and importance of "mottainai" in the story. (Source: Anime News Network)

title: Mountain
synopsis: Short film directed by Kenji Iwaisawa, adapted from the manga of Hiroyuki Ohashi.

tags.name: Surreal Comedy
tags.description: Prominently features comedic moments that defy casual reasoning, resulting in illogical events.

title: masui
synopsis: The animation depicts the strange feeling of sleepiness while bathing in the bathtub, the gradual slipping away of consciousness from the body, and the awakening from this situation as an unreal experience hidden in daily life.

title: Qing Chi Hong Xiaodou Ba! Wai Zhuan: Mo Xiang Wang

title: Taiko no Tatsujin (2022)
synopsis: Taiko lover Kotarou from Taiko Village travels to Omiko City with his partner Don-chan in order to spread the greatness of Taiko across the world.

title: Tokyu Plaza Ginza Open
synopsis: A commercial made to advertise the grand opening of the Tokyu Plaza Ginza, a giant indoor shopping mall with restaurants, cafes, boutiques, furiniture, and spas.

title: Mr. Deniroo IN HENTEKO MUSHI
synopsis: Mr. Deniroo is an alien from the outer space, his space ship is broken and he can't come home his home planet. He encounters various Earth things. A short movie directed by Tatsushi Momen (KOO-KI). (Source: Official Viemo Channel)

title: Norasco
synopsis: Follows an easygoing Scottish Fold's mundane everyday life.

title: Fourth Period Swimming Class
synopsis: The fourth-grade protagonist is going through physical and emotional changes and feels awkward as she can’t help but compare herself to her friends. One day, she is blown away by her classmate Aya’s swimming and realizes that she has feelings for her. (Source: Geidai Animation)

tags.name: LGBTQ+ Themes

title: Fussa Dancing Animation
titleNative: 福生ダンシングアニメーション
synopsis: "This work was produced at a workshop where you can experience the animation created using a technique called rotoscoping up close at the event "Ginza Shoeikai Art Festival" held in Fussa, Tokyo in June 2014. We asked the visitors to draw each one by hand, and joined them together to complete the work." (Source: Official YouTube Upload, translated)

title: First Hand Memories
synopsis: A girl looks at the surrounding world through a toilet paper roll, a play everybody has similar experience of. The Rakugo story of PARROT is told by a Rakugo-ka, or a lone storyteller formed of Japanese characters. 1st year work 2016 by Haruka Umemura, Tokyo University of the Arts, Graduate School of Film and New Media, Department of Animation. (Soruce: Geidai Animation)

title: Tomo ni Kagayaku Ashita no Tame ni.
titleRomaji: Tomo ni Kagayaku Ashita no Tame ni.
titleNative: ともに輝く明日のために
synopsis: The connection between Hokkaido's harsh nature and its warm people. Through the story of a young woman searching for her own path despite her uncertainty, the film depicts "a brighter tomorrow together." With these words as its purpose, the HEPCO Group will continue to support the daily lives of the people of Hokkaido and the future of life on this land. We hope this animation will light a small flame in your heart.'' (Source: Official Youtube, translated)

title: Sora Iro Hana Iro
synopsis: A woman and a dog go on a spiritual journey and undergo a spiritual transformation while wandering in a forest side-by-side. Sora Iro Hana Iro is not a straight-forward narrative story, but rather series of subjective impressions to tell us more about the state of mind of the female protagonist. (Source: Nishikata Film Review)

tags.name: Stop Motion
tags.description: Animation style characterized by physical objects being moved incrementally between frames to create the illusion of movement.

title: Rain shower
synopsis: It happens at a half-demolished shopping street. After a brief rain, there comes a moment of another landscape. 1st year work 2015, Tokyo University of the Arts, Graduate School of Film and New Media, Department of Animation. (Source: Geidai Animation)

title: Cinnamon no Genki ni o Dekake
titleRomaji: Cinnamon no Genki ni o Dekake
titleNative: シナモンのげんきにおでかけ
synopsis: Cinnamoroll teaches politeness and discipline in a fun way! This OVA features lessons on getting dressed, greeting people, using a knife and fork, being nice, and a silly guessing game!

title: Japanese Chess
synopsis: At a Shogi-chess game, the players start pondering opponent’s game strategy or calculations. They lose themselves in their deep contemplation. 1st year work 2016 by Moe Kuribara, Tokyo University of the Arts, Graduate School of Film and New Media, Department of Animation. (Source: Geidai Animation)

title: Watakushiritori
synopsis: One day is presented by shiritori, a word game of last and first. A chain of images in one line evolves the story of this game. Graduate Work 2013, Tokyo University of the Arts, Graduate School of Film and New Media, Department of Animation. (Source: Geidai Animation)

title: Gekkan! Nanmono Anime
titleNative: げっかん！なんものアニメ
synonyms: なんでもいきもの　げっかん！なんものアニメ
synopsis: A new short anime series based on the characters "Nandemo Ikimono" created by illustrator and character designer Yuri Yokomizo. The stories follow the lives of any and all things, from animals and plants to food and objects. The shorts will stream monthly on various social media services such as Twitter, Instagram, YouTube, and TikTok, as well as on ABEMA. (Source: Anime News Network, edited)

title: My Father’s Room
synopsis: She was abused by her father during childhood. Since he left, the pain and anger had begun to fade. One day, unexpectedly, she was struck by a revelation about her father's life that cast her feelings about him into confusion. (Source: Vimeo)
